body.title: CLOSED: Per-minion feature branch workspaces — local clone + branch + merge-back
body.result: MinionWorkspace clone/branch/merge-back in lab/claudecode/minion_workspace.py; 16 tests pass; sprint skill integration deferred to skill-edit pass
body.description:
When multiple minions work concurrently, the single-working-tree assumption breaks (stash/pull/pop only isolates serially). Adopt per-minion workspace clones with feature branches; merge back via local git (no GitHub round-trip required, though optional).

**Two-piece design (split if either grows):**
1. Per-minion workspace clone + branch convention (small infra win on its own)
2. Merge-back protocol (who does it? on what trigger? auto vs. review-gated?)

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/minion_workspace.py (NEW — clone/branch/merge helpers), agent_datacenter (workspace path convention — overlaps T-agent-datacenter-workspaces), /home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint (branch-per-claim), /home/akien/.claude/skills/sprint-batch (same)
**Design rules:** feature-branch-per-minion (no shared working tree); local clone destination is per-minion (e.g., ~/.agent_datacenter/<instance>/workspace/TheIgors); merge-back is to local main, push to GitHub remains explicit. T-docs-live-in-code: minion_workspace.py docstring documents the clone/branch/merge contract.
**Scope boundary:** IN: clone helper, branch naming convention (e.g., minion/<instance>/<ticket-id>), merge-back command, sprint skill integration. IN: works without GitHub (local git clone+merge). OUT: GitHub PR-per-minion workflow (that's a separate optional layer). OUT: conflict-resolution automation (manual for now). OUT: workspace cleanup/GC.
**Test plan:** unit tests for minion_workspace.py (clone creates branch, merge-back applies cleanly, conflict detection raises). Manual integration check: spin up two simulated minions, both claim different tickets, verify they don't collide. Acceptance: two concurrent minions can ship cleanly without stash dance.
